Kerberoastable.tooltip: This account exposes a Service Principal Name. Any authenticated user can request a service ticket for it and crack the account password offline. (servicePrincipalName attribute)

PasswordNotRequired.tooltip: The account is allowed to have an empty password. An attacker who learns the username can authenticate with no credential at all. (userAccountControl bit PASSWD_NOTREQD 0x0020)

PasswordNeverExpires.tooltip: The password of this account never expires. Long-lived credentials are higher value to attackers and bypass your password rotation policy. (userAccountControl bit DONT_EXPIRE_PASSWORD 0x10000)

ReversibleEncryption.tooltip: The password of this account is stored in a form equivalent to plaintext. Anyone who can read the directory database recovers the password. (userAccountControl bit ENCRYPTED_TEXT_PWD_ALLOWED 0x0080)

AsRepRoastable.tooltip: Kerberos pre-authentication is disabled for this account. An unauthenticated attacker can request an AS-REP and crack the account password offline. (userAccountControl bit DONT_REQUIRE_PREAUTH 0x400000)

UnconstrainedDelegation.tooltip: This computer can impersonate any user that authenticates against it. A compromise of this host compromises every account that ever logged on. (userAccountControl bit TRUSTED_FOR_DELEGATION 0x80000)

ConstrainedDelegation.tooltip: This computer can impersonate users when authenticating to: {{targets}}. Verify each target SPN is still required and minimal. (msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo)

Rbcd.tooltip: These principals can impersonate users to this computer: {{principals}}. Often legitimate (e.g. SQL Server delegation), but if a low-privilege account has write access to this attribute it enables a relay attack. (msDS-AllowedToActOnBehalfOfOtherIdentity)
